question:
I'm new to this codebase and need a real code-based tour before my first behavior change.

Find the central runtime flow, trace it from the public API to the code that does the work, and show the nearby code paths and tests I should read next.

graph.answer:
The central runtime flow is:

- Public entrypoint: [`index.js`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/express/index.js#L11) re-exports [`lib/express.js`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/express/lib/express.js#L27).
- App factory: [`lib/express.js`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/express/lib/express.js#L36) builds a callable app function, mixes in `EventEmitter` plus the app prototype, then attaches `express.request` and `express.response` as per-app prototypes.
- App setup: [`lib/application.js`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/express/lib/application.js#L59) initializes defaults and lazily creates `this.router` the first time it is needed.
- Request dispatch: [`lib/application.js`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/express/lib/application.js#L152) is the real per-request work path. It sets `X-Powered-By`, links `req.res` / `res.req`, swaps prototypes onto `req` and `res`, initializes `res.locals`, then delegates to `this.router.handle(req, res, done)`.
- Mounting and route registration: [`lib/application.js`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/express/lib/application.js#L190) handles `app.use()`, including mounted sub-apps; [`lib/application.js`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/express/lib/application.js#L256) handles `app.route()`. The generated HTTP verb methods are wired at [`lib/application.js`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/express/lib/application.js#L471), and `app.all()` is at [`lib/application.js`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/express/lib/application.js#L494).
- Server startup: [`lib/application.js`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/express/lib/application.js#L598) wraps the app in `http.createServer(this)`.

The important boundary: the actual routing engine lives in the external `router` package, not in this repo. This repo’s runtime responsibility is to set up app/request/response state and hand off to that router cleanly.

What to read next, in order:

1. [`test/exports.js`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/express/test/exports.js#L7)  
   Confirms the public surface: `Router`, `json/raw/static/text/urlencoded`, `application`, `request`, and `response`.

2. [`test/app.js`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/express/test/app.js#L7)  
   Covers the app object itself: callable function, `EventEmitter` inheritance, 404-without-routes, `parent`, `mountpath`, `path()`, and env-driven defaults.

3. [`test/app.request.js`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/express/test/app.request.js#L8) and [`test/app.response.js`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/express/test/app.response.js#L8)  
   These prove the prototype wiring done in `app.handle()` and how sub-app inheritance/override works.

4. [`test/app.use.js`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/express/test/app.use.js#L21) and [`test/app.use.js`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/express/test/app.use.js#L258)  
   Best next read for middleware flow: mounted apps, path stripping, arrays/nesting, and edge cases around `app.use(path, middleware)`.

5. [`test/app.route.js`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/express/test/app.route.js#L6)  
   Shows how `app.route()` composes handlers and how promise rejection is handled at route level.

6. [`test/app.router.js`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/express/test/app.router.js#L11)  
   This is the strongest “behavior change” map for the dispatch path: methods, param decoding, strict/case routing, `next()`, `next('route')`, `next('router')`, errors, and promise support. The most useful anchors inside it are the sections starting at lines 39, 94, 287, 423, 818, 854, 879, 903, and 965.

7. [`test/app.routes.error.js`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/express/test/app.routes.error.js#L8)  
   Focused on error propagation through route callbacks, which is the branch most people miss first.

8. [`test/Router.js`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/express/test/Router.js#L9) and [`test/Route.js`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/express/test/Route.js#L9)  
   These are the lower-level semantics and performance tests for the router/route machinery. If your change affects matching or stack traversal, read these next.

9. [`test/app.listen.js`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/express/test/app.listen.js#L6)  
   Confirms the server wrapper behavior around `http.createServer(app)`.

If you want, I can turn this into a “first-change checklist” for a specific kind of change, like middleware, routing, errors, or request/response helpers.
